method_atom: A change-of-variables (coordinate-transformation) technique that maps a finite region of space to a point while preserving the form of the governing wave equation, so that an incident field passes around the enclosed region with vanishing scattering and no distortion outside. The transformation forces the effective constitutive parameters of the surrounding shell to be spatially varying and anisotropic; if one parameter is held isotropic a conjugate parameter must diverge at the inner boundary, and this divergence is removed only by admitting full anisotropy in the second parameter, whose values follow explicitly from a symmetric deformation gradient or otherwise from a stress-like tensor satisfying a static equilibrium equation.
object_atom: Acoustic (sound) waves propagating through a fluid or solid medium, and a physical shell of engineered material — a pentamode material with anisotropic mass density and mechanical stiffness — that envelops an object to make it acoustically invisible in 2D and 3D.
brief_reason: The transformation/change-of-variables scattering-cancellation machinery and its anisotropic-parameter constraints carry across fields, while the sound-wave-in-a-medium cloak built from pentamode material is the acoustics-specific object.
